claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 3 children:
  - Alex is 3 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 2 years old and lives with you.
  - Charlie is 3 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 2 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Charlie:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 3 (under 16)
  - Someone who lives with the child is already claiming
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your situation, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first one:

**Do you have any children under 16 years old, or children aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have three children - Alex who is 3 years old, Blake who is 2, and Charlie who is also 3 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have three children: Alex (3 years old), Blake (2 years old), and Charlie (3 years old).

Now, let me ask you about your living situation:

**Do you live in the UK, and do all three of your children live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I live in the UK, in Birmingham. Blake lives with me at home, but Alex and Charlie don't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. So Blake lives with you in Birmingham, but Alex and Charlie live elsewhere.

Let me ask about Alex and Charlie:

**How long ago did Alex and Charlie go to live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex and Charlie have been living elsewhere for a while now - it's been more than 8 weeks for both of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since it's been more than 8 weeks for both Alex and Charlie, I need to understand if you're still contributing to their upkeep.

**Are you making any financial contributions towards Alex and Charlie's care, such as paying for their food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute to both of their upkeep. I help with things like clothes and other essentials for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. I need to understand the level of your contributions a bit better:

**Are your contributions to Alex and Charlie's upkeep at least equal to the current Child Benefit payment amount (which is £23.80 per week for the first child and £15.90 per week for each additional child)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I contribute at least that amount for both Alex and Charlie. For each of them, I'm paying around £26 per week towards their upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now let me ask about Blake, who lives with you:

**Are you responsible for Blake's care and living expenses, and is anyone else (such as your local council) paying towards Blake's accommodation or maintenance?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I'm responsible for Blake's care and all their living expenses. No one else is paying towards their accommodation or maintenance - it's just me looking after them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that clarification. Now I have one more question about your circumstances:

**Do you live in the UK as a British citizen, or do you have a different immigration status (such as settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another visa)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'm a British citizen living here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you for providing all that information. I now have what I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me review your situation:

- **Blake (2 years old)**: Lives with you in Birmingham, you are responsible for their care and all living expenses, and no one else is contributing. You are a British citizen living in the UK.

- **Alex (3 years old)**: Has been living elsewhere for more than 8 weeks, but you are contributing at least £26 per week towards their upkeep (which exceeds the Child Benefit payment threshold).

- **Charlie (3 years old)**: Has been living elsewhere for more than 8 weeks, but you are contributing at least £26 per week towards their upkeep (which exceeds the Child Benefit payment threshold).

Based on the Child Benefit eligibility rules, I can now provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Great news!** You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three of your children:

- **Blake** ✓ Eligible
- **Alex** ✓ Eligible  
- **Charlie** ✓ Eligible

Your eligibility for Blake is straightforward as they live with you and you are their primary carer. For Alex and Charlie, even though they live with someone else beyond the initial 8-week period, your consistent financial contributions of £26 per week for each child exceeds the Child Benefit payment thresholds, which allows you to continue claiming for them.

When you're ready to claim, you can do so through the official Child Benefit application process. Remember, only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child, so if anyone else is also claiming for any of these children, you'll need to sort that out between you or HMRC will decide.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about your eligibility?

reasonings: Blake is eligible because: (1) Blake is under 16 years old, (2) Blake lives with you in the UK, (3) You are responsible for Blake's care and living expenses, (4) You are a British citizen, and (5) No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake. You meet all the basic eligibility criteria.

Alex is eligible because: (1) Alex is under 16 years old, (2) Although Alex lives with someone else (more than 8 weeks), you continue to contribute at least £26 per week towards their upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit payment amount of £23.80 per week, (3) You are a British citizen living in the UK, and (4) The rules state you can continue to receive Child Benefit for more than 8 weeks if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards the child's upkeep.

Charlie is eligible because: (1) Charlie is under 16 years old, (2) Although Charlie lives with someone else (more than 8 weeks), you continue to contribute at least £26 per week towards their upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit payment amount of £15.90 per week for additional children, (3) You are a British citizen living in the UK, and (4) The rules state you can continue to receive Child Benefit for more than 8 weeks if you contribute at least the equivalent of the Child Benefit payment amount towards the child's upkeep.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for all three children. Blake is eligible as they live with you and you are their primary carer. Alex and Charlie are eligible because although they live with someone else and it has been more than 8 weeks, your regular financial contributions towards their upkeep (£26 per week for each) meet or exceed the Child Benefit payment thresholds, which allows you to continue receiving payments for them. As a British citizen living in the UK with responsibility for all three children and making appropriate contributions, you meet the eligibility criteria for all three.

You meet all the basic eligibility criteria.

Alex and Charlie are eligible because although they live with someone else and it has been more than 8 weeks, your regular financial contributions towards their upkeep (£26 per week for each) meet or exceed the Child Benefit payment thresholds, which allows you to continue receiving payments for them.
